evidence_quotes.quote: Increasingly, these practicesoften enforced through manipulative user interface designs or dark patternsdeprive buyers of true ownership and diminish consumer trust.
The current system for digital game ownership is extremely problematic and fails to protect the fundamental rights of consumers.

paragraphs.text: The current system for digital game ownership is extremely problematic and fails to protect the fundamental rights of consumers. Companies are permitted to revoke access to digital games and other software arbitrarily, meaning that a purchase does not guarantee long-term use or ownership. The industrys excessive reliance on restrictive licenses and required online accounts enables publishers to remove products from a consumers library at will, sometimes even erasing entire catalogs without offering any offline functionality, refunds, or alternative access. Increasingly, these practicesoften enforced through manipulative user interface designs or dark patternsdeprive buyers of true ownership and diminish consumer trust. Legal frameworks must be strengthened so that a purchased product remains accessible to the owner, including by mandating preservation options or offline play once support ends. Digital content should not be subject to arbitrary revocation, and robust regulation is needed to protect both the value and the accessibility of what consumers pay for in the digital marketplace.
